items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP at bar close; require volume on entry bar >= 1.2x 20-day avg intraday volume at that time increment. Entry valid between 09:35–10:15 ET only.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.2% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: preserves RL-002 (max 0.35% account risk per trade) at QUARTER position size.
items.target: +2.0% from entry price (R:R ~1.67:1 vs 1.2% stop). Rationale: short-duration energy trade; crude-correlated upside capped given stub thesis and REDUCE_ONLY regime.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY circuit breaker active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. Thesis is a manual stub with no live research; bull_pct=50 is placeholder, not signal-derived. XOM highly correlated to crude; any crude reversal invalidates bull lean immediately. Stub conviction is not verified by fundamentals or technicals.

items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP at bar close; volume on entry bar >= 1.2x 20-day avg. Entry valid between 09:35–10:15 ET. CVX tends to track XOM — do not enter both simultaneously if portfolio open-risk approaches RL-003 threshold.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.2% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: same RL-002 discipline; CVX lower vol than OXY so tighter stop defensible.
items.target: +2.0% from entry (R:R ~1.67:1). Rationale: energy major, correlated with XOM; separate position only if sector exposure does not double-count beyond RL-003.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. Stub thesis only. CVX and XOM highly correlated; holding both simultaneously compounds sector concentration risk and may approach RL-003. Risk Agent should flag if both triggered.

items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP; volume >= 1.3x 20-day avg (higher threshold than majors due to OXY's elevated intraday vol). Entry valid 09:35–10:00 ET only — tighter window given volatility profile.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.5% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: OXY ATR is meaningfully wider than XOM/CVX; stop must accommodate noise without exceeding RL-002 at QUARTER size.
items.target: +2.5% from entry (R:R ~1.67:1 vs 1.5% stop). Rationale: higher beta vs majors justifies wider target.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. OXY is the highest-beta name in this energy cluster; stub thesis makes conviction unverified. Elevated crude sensitivity means intraday energy reversals hit OXY hardest. Combined sector position with XOM/CVX risks breaching RL-003.

items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP; volume >= 1.2x 20-day avg. Entry valid 09:35–10:30 ET (slightly wider window acceptable for ETF due to lower idiosyncratic risk). XLE preferred over single-name energy if RL-003 open-risk headroom is limited.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.0% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: ETF structure reduces single-name gap risk; tighter stop appropriate.
items.target: +1.8% from entry (R:R ~1.8:1). Rationale: ETF cap on upside vs single names; lower vol profile.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. XLE is the sector ETF and lowest-fragility energy pick given diversification. Stub thesis only. If single-name energy positions (XOM, CVX, OXY) are already open, prefer XLE as final energy allocation to avoid RL-003 breach.

items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP; volume >= 1.4x 20-day avg (CCL requires stronger volume confirmation given macro rate sensitivity and elevated short interest). Entry valid 09:35–10:00 ET only.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.8% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: CCL has wider ATR than energy majors; stop must absorb noise while respecting RL-002 at QUARTER size.
items.target: +3.0% from entry (R:R ~1.67:1 vs 1.8% stop). Rationale: consumer discretionary / cruise names need larger moves to justify entries given macro headwinds.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. CCL is a high-macro-sensitivity, high-debt consumer discretionary. Stub thesis with no fundamental backing is especially fragile here — real thesis would require rate environment, fuel cost, and booking trend analysis. Treat as lowest-confidence pick in this set. Rate spike, broad market weakness, or fuel spike are rapid invalidators.

items.trigger: First 5-min candle close above premarket high AND price above VWAP; volume >= 1.2x 20-day avg. Entry valid 09:35–10:15 ET. EBAY is more idiosyncratic vs energy cluster — preferred if portfolio already has high energy concentration.
items.stop: Hard stop at 1.3% below entry price OR below premarket session low, whichever is tighter. Rationale: EBAY is lower-vol e-commerce; tighter stop vs CCL appropriate.
items.target: +2.2% from entry (R:R ~1.69:1). Rationale: EBAY moves modestly without catalyst; target sized to realistic intraday range.
items.note: REDUCE_ONLY active — QUARTER sizing mandatory. Stub thesis only — no research on current EBAY fundamentals, competitive position, or near-term catalysts. EBAY is most idiosyncratic of the 6 candidates; stub data is least informative here. Any untracked company-specific event is an unquantified risk. Prefer as diversifier only if energy cluster positions are already near RL-004 limit.
